title: Sharp Chest Pain - Acute Pericarditis
chief_complaint: Sharp chest pain for 2 days, worse when lying down and with deep breathing, relieved by sitting forward

physical_exam.heart: Pericardial friction rub heard at left sternal border

hidden_data.ecg: Diffuse ST elevation in multiple leads with PR depression in II

patient_responses.pain_onset: Started 2 days ago after a flu-like illness
patient_responses.pain_character: Sharp, stabbing pain, worse when I lie flat
patient_responses.relieving_factor: It feels better when I sit up and lean forward

hints.ecg_hint: Look at ALL leads - is the ST elevation localized or diffuse?
hints.exam_hint: Listen carefully at the left sternal border

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! You correctly identified pericarditis. The diffuse ST elevation and PR depression are classic.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is acute pericarditis, not what you diagnosed. Key features: diffuse ST elevation (not localized like STEMI), PR depression, positional pain.

ecg.comment: Essential for diagnosis. Shows diffuse ST elevation.

troponin.comment: Important to check for myocardial involvement.

cxr.comment: Reasonable to rule out other causes.

echo.comment: Important to assess for pericardial effusion.

feedback.attending_note: Good case, Doctor. Remember: NSAIDs + Colchicine for 3 months is first-line. Avoid steroids in initial episodes as they increase recurrence risk.
